question.text: The formula for calculating tick value in commodity futures is:

answer.text: Tick Value = (Lot size / Quotation factor) × Tick size
Tick Value = Lot size × Quotation factor × Tick size
Tick Value = Tick size / Lot size
Tick Value = Lot size × Tick size

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Tick Value = (Lot size / Quotation factor) × Tick size. The quotation factor converts the lot size to the same units as the price quote. **The Trap:** Option (b) multiplies instead of dividing by quotation factor. Option (c) gives a tiny value. Option (d) ignores the quotation factor entirely. **Cross-Exam Context:** Tick value concept is specific to commodity derivatives due to varying lot sizes and quotation units. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Understanding tick value is essential for risk management — it tells you the exact P&L impact of the minimum price movement on your position.
